no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly dictates the exact implementation: specific variable names (retries_ctl, max_retries, initial_backoff_ms, max_backoff_ms), exact catch syntax ('catch (const zkutil::KeeperException & e)'), exact method calls (retries_ctl.retryLoop, function_names_and_asts.clear()), exact log message text ('Keeper hardware error'), and exact include files. solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on gold-specific implementation details: exact variable name 'retries_ctl' (test_zookeeper_retries_control_usage asserts 'ZooKeeperRetriesControl retries_ctl('), exact parameter names 'initial_backoff_ms' and 'max_backoff_ms', exact string 'retries_ctl.retryLoop', exact catch syntax 'catch (const zkutil::KeeperException & e)', exact log message 'Keeper hardware error'. tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Every fail-to-pass test is purely text-matching: reading the file content and asserting substrings exist (e.g., 'assert "#include <Common/ZooKeeper/ZooKeeperCommon.h>" in content'). No test compiles, executes, or calls any code. The pass-to-pass tests do use subprocess (grep, git) but only for style checks, not behavioral verification.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub implementation that simply appends the required strings as comments or dead code to the file would pass all f2p tests. For example, adding the include lines, a comment block containing all the required strings ('catch (const zkutil::KeeperException & e)', 'isHardwareError(e.code)', 'ZooKeeperRetriesControl retries_ctl(', etc.) without any actual functional code would satisfy every assertion.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution script is at /workspace/clickhouse/solution/solve.sh (or mounted in the container). The agent could read the test file at tests/test_outputs.py to discover all exact strings being asserted, then simply insert those strings into the target file. The tests only check for string presence, making this trivially exploitable.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with descriptive function names (test_hardware_error_include, test_keeper_exception_handler, test_retry_loop_pattern, etc.) and docstrings explaining what each test verifies.
